Several [minor league baseball](/wiki/Minor_league_baseball "Minor league baseball") teams have been based in [Salinas, California](/wiki/Salinas,_California "Salinas, California") and played in the [California League](/wiki/California_League "California League").

## Salinas Spurs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Salinas_Spurs&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Salinas Spurs")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Salinas_Spurs "Special:EditPage/Salinas Spurs") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Salinas Spurs"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Salinas+Spurs%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Salinas+Spurs%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Salinas+Spurs%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Salinas+Spurs%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Salinas+Spurs%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Salinas+Spurs%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(September 2020)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

The **Salinas Spurs** were a [California League](/wiki/California_League "California League") team that played from 1982 to 1987 and again from 1989 to 1992. Located in [Salinas, California](/wiki/Salinas,_California "Salinas, California"), they were affiliated with the [Chicago Cubs](/wiki/Chicago_Cubs "Chicago Cubs") from 1982 to 1983, the [Seattle Mariners](/wiki/Seattle_Mariners "Seattle Mariners") from 1984 to 1987, and in 1989 they had a partial working agreement with the [San Francisco Giants](/wiki/San_Francisco_Giants "San Francisco Giants"). That season, they had seven Japanese players play for them.

They did not play as the Salinas Spurs in 1988, because they moved to [Riverside, California](/wiki/Riverside,_California "Riverside, California") to become the [Riverside Red Wave](/wiki/Riverside_Red_Wave "Riverside Red Wave"). Baseball returned to Salinas after the [Fresno Suns](/wiki/Fresno_Suns "Fresno Suns") moved there for the 1989 season and became the Spurs.
Their last affiliation (a partial one) was with the [Chicago White Sox](/wiki/Chicago_White_Sox "Chicago White Sox") in their final season in 1992, when they had half the roster of Japanese players with agreements with Japanese professional baseball leagues.

## Salinas Angels

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Salinas_Spurs&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Salinas Angels")]

The **Salinas Angels** were a [California League](/wiki/California_League "California League") baseball team based in [Salinas, California](/wiki/Salinas,_California "Salinas, California"), USA that played from 1976 to 1980. They played their home games at [Salinas Municipal Stadium](/wiki/Salinas_Municipal_Stadium "Salinas Municipal Stadium").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The team made the California League playoffs in 1976 and 1977, reaching (and losing) the league finals in each of those seasons.

Each season, the team had a new manager. The team's managers, by year: [Del Crandall](/wiki/Del_Crandall "Del Crandall") (1976), [Moose Stubing](/wiki/Moose_Stubing "Moose Stubing") (1977), [Chuck Cottier](/wiki/Chuck_Cottier "Chuck Cottier") (1978), [Chris Cannizzaro](/wiki/Chris_Cannizzaro "Chris Cannizzaro") (1979) and [Tom Zimmer](/wiki/Tom_Zimmer "Tom Zimmer") (1980).

Notable players include major league All-Stars [Tom Brunansky](/wiki/Tom_Brunansky "Tom Brunansky"), [Mark Clear](/wiki/Mark_Clear "Mark Clear"), [Dave Engle](/wiki/Dave_Engle "Dave Engle"), [Ken Schrom](/wiki/Ken_Schrom "Ken Schrom"), [Dickie Thon](/wiki/Dickie_Thon "Dickie Thon") and [Mike Witt](/wiki/Mike_Witt "Mike Witt").

## Salinas Packers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Salinas_Spurs&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Salinas Packers")]

There were two incarnations of the [baseball](/wiki/Baseball "Baseball") team known as the **Salinas Packers**. The first existed from 1954 to 1958, and was affiliated with the [Pittsburgh Pirates](/wiki/Pittsburgh_Pirates "Pittsburgh Pirates") in 1955 and the [Milwaukee Braves](/wiki/Milwaukee_Braves "Milwaukee Braves") from 1956 to 1958. The second incarnation existed from 1973 to 1975, and was affiliated with the [California Angels](/wiki/California_Angels "California Angels"). They played their home games at [Municipal Stadium](/wiki/Municipal_Stadium_(Salinas) "Municipal Stadium (Salinas)") in the second incarnation.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Both incarnations were located in [Salinas, California](/wiki/Salinas,_California "Salinas, California") and played in the [California League](/wiki/California_League "California League").

In 1957, they won the California League title. One of their players, [John Balaz](/wiki/John_Balaz "John Balaz"), was named MVP in 1973. In 1976, they were renamed the [Salinas Angels](/wiki/Salinas_Angels "Salinas Angels").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Salinas Indians

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Salinas_Spurs&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Salinas Indians")]

The **Salinas Indians** were a [California League](/wiki/California_League "California League") baseball team based in [Salinas, California](/wiki/Salinas,_California "Salinas, California"), USA that played in 1965. They were managed by [Phil Cavarretta](/wiki/Phil_Cavarretta "Phil Cavarretta") and were affiliated with the [Cleveland Indians](/wiki/Cleveland_Indians "Cleveland Indians"). Their home stadium was [Salinas Municipal Stadium](/wiki/Salinas_Municipal_Stadium "Salinas Municipal Stadium").

Multiple major leaguers played for the team, including [Frank Baker](/wiki/Frank_Baker_(outfielder) "Frank Baker (outfielder)"), [Gary Boyd](/wiki/Gary_Boyd_(baseball) "Gary Boyd (baseball)"), [Larry Foster](/wiki/Larry_Foster_(baseball) "Larry Foster (baseball)"), [Gomer Hodge](/wiki/Gomer_Hodge "Gomer Hodge"), [Ray Miller](/wiki/Ray_Miller_(baseball_manager) "Ray Miller (baseball manager)"), [Dave Nelson](/wiki/Dave_Nelson "Dave Nelson"), [Sam Parrilla](/wiki/Sam_Parrilla "Sam Parrilla"), [Richie Scheinblum](/wiki/Richie_Scheinblum "Richie Scheinblum") and [Oscar Zamora](/wiki/Oscar_Zamora_(baseball) "Oscar Zamora (baseball)").[[3]](#cite_note-3)
